name: MicrosoftDefenderCLI
description: Run the Microsoft Defender CLI for security analysis with support for filesystem, container image, and AI model scanning.
helpMarkDown: Runs the Microsoft Defender CLI for security analysis. Supports filesystem scanning, container image scanning, and AI model scanning with SARIF output format.
author: Microsoft Corporation

inputs.name: command
inputs.label: Scan Type
inputs.helpMarkDown: The type of defender scan to perform. Default: Filesystem Scan

inputs.name: fileSystemPath
inputs.label: Filesystem Path
inputs.helpMarkDown: The filesystem path to scan for security vulnerabilities. Defaults to the repository root directory.

inputs.name: imageName
inputs.label: Container Image Name
inputs.helpMarkDown: The container image name to scan (e.g., nginx:latest, myregistry.com/myapp:v1.0)

inputs.name: modelPath
inputs.label: AI Model Path or URL
inputs.helpMarkDown: The path to the AI model file/directory or a URL (http:// or https://) pointing to the model to scan for security vulnerabilities.

inputs.name: policy
inputs.label: Policy
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of a well known Microsoft policy. If no configuration is provided, the policy may instruct the Defender CLI what tools to run. Default: azuredevops

inputs.name: args
inputs.label: Additional Arguments
inputs.helpMarkDown: Additional arguments to pass to the Defender CLI. These will be appended to the command as-is.

inputs.name: pr-summary
inputs.label: Post Vulnerability Summary
inputs.helpMarkDown: Add summary of new vulnerabilities to pipeline summary.

inputs.name: break
inputs.label: Fail on Critical Vulnerabilities
inputs.helpMarkDown: When checked, the pipeline will fail if critical vulnerabilities are found.
